prompt:
The relay client's public session header still publishes the complete mutable `struct session`. Several consumers now reach into its counters, phase, owned peer string, and allocator hooks directly, so an otherwise internal representation has become a source-level contract. Refactor the session into a genuinely opaque handle: `include/session.h` must expose only the forward declaration `typedef struct session session;`, the sole structure definition must live in `src/session.c`, and the in-tree pump and adapter must use the existing accessors and mutators instead of fields. Keep the current public function signatures and enum values unchanged.

This is an encapsulation change, not an external format or lifecycle redesign. `adapter/session_wire.h` is the deployed adapter contract: `adapter_session_v1` must remain exactly 40 bytes with the same offsets and byte-for-byte little-endian snapshot encoding. Preserve adapter validation and truncation as well: null arguments return -1, a rejected snapshot leaves a non-null output untouched, and peer names longer than 16 bytes are truncated to their first 16 bytes. Preserve pump gating and traffic accounting. Preserve creation behavior as well: with a supplied allocator, creation allocates the handle first and the NUL-terminated peer copy second; failure of the first allocation returns NULL without releasing anything, while failure of the peer allocation releases the handle exactly once. Successful destruction releases the peer copy and then the handle through the allocator captured at creation, and destroying NULL remains a no-op.

Run `make test`. Makefile, .gitignore, mintest.h, opaque_probe.c, session_api_probe.c, source_contract.sh, test_session.c, adapter/session_wire.h, include/session_adapter.h, and include/session_pump.h are protected and must not be edited.

full_distill.primary_skill: encapsulate a mutable C object behind an opaque handle and migrate internal consumers to accessors without changing an external binary record or ownership semantics
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits 2 because the protected opaque_probe.c can compile direct reads of session fields, proving that include/session.h still exposes the mutable representation
full_distill.success_contract: the public session type is incomplete and defined only in src/session.c, all existing session behavior remains reachable through the existing API, adapter_session_v1 stays byte-exact, both injected allocation failures unwind identically, and teardown releases peer then handle exactly once
adversarial_factors: a protected negative-compilation probe dereferences id, phase, peer, counters, and allocator fields, so cosmetic typedef aliases or a read-only comment do not make the handle opaque
a protected positive-compilation probe pins the struct-tag alias, every session function type, and all three enum values while the negative probe requires the resulting type to be incomplete

the pump and adapter currently use direct fields and therefore stop compiling when the definition moves; a protected source-contract check also requires the sole definition and the complete migration to existing accessors and mutators
the protected adapter contract asserts sizeof, every field offset, and a complete 40-byte little-endian snapshot, rejecting layout drift and native-endian shortcuts
a deterministic allocator fails the first and second allocations separately and records unwind calls, rejecting changed allocation order, extra allocations, leaks, and release-on-first-failure

successful teardown mutates the caller's allocator after creation, records pointer order, and repeats the NULL case, rejecting borrowed allocator state, handle-first destruction, use-after-free of allocator hooks, double release, and non-null-safe cleanup
